fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Maine Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Maine - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity maine, how to transfer occupational therapist license to maine, occupational therapist compact maine, maine occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Maine Board of Occupational Therapy Practice
board.parent_dept: Maine Office of Professional and Occupational Regulation (OPOR)
board.mailing_address:
Maine Board of Occupational Therapy Practice
35 State House Station
Augusta, ME 04333-0035

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT)

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Maine.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create a MyLicense Account
steps.description: Register for a new account on the Maine Department of Professional & Financial Regulation's MyLicense online portal to begin your application.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your MyLicense account, select 'Apply for a New License,' and complete the Occupational Therapist application form with all required personal and professional information.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary documents through the MyLicense portal, including official transcripts verifying your degree, and verification of your National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) certification.

steps.title: Complete Maine Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Pass the Maine jurisprudence examination, which assesses your knowledge of the state's laws and rules pertaining to occupational therapy practice.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $50.00 through the MyLicense portal to finalize your submission.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Maine OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Maine have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Maine has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Maine?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Maine while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Maine
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Maine, you must use the state's online application system, MyLicense, operated by the Department of Professional & Financial Regulation.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the MyLicense portal, click 'Register a New Account,' and follow the prompts to create your username and password. Once registered, log in to begin the application process.
